reviews.review_msg: Only share the objective news views in class, never taught how to write a good business writing. Rubric is not clear and the group projects are totally not useful for the future upper level BUS Studies.

It felt like many of the lectures consisted of just Ilia conversing with a rotation of the same few students. AI was heavily encouraged in this class, but using it to your advantage was not taught. The structure of the class doesn't really teach you how to think critically, but instead, how to depend on AI for higher grades.

Ilia was pretty good overall, he's strict but pretty nice and understanding if you talk to him. The TA, Olessya, was the worst TA I've had. Her grading was ridiculously hard, she gave no meaningful feedback, and she would talk the whole time through our timed tutorial assignments, which were hard enough without the distraction and pressure.

Ilia himself is a very nice person and will explain concepts you don't understand if you ask him nicely. the TAs are the WORST enemy because your entire grade depends on them. Olessya A was the worst TA marker and marks like you ate her dog or something. Was 1 percent from the next letter grade so that explains a lot

Ilia himself is a bit intimidating, but he's actually very nice/approachable in office hours. He's not overly accessible outside of class though. I hated this class, and much of that is due to how the 217 is designed. I don't think anything we learned in class was helpful for assignments. Also beware that everything is about AI.
he works for netanyahu

Although many people say he can be intimidating and "hates every sole on Earth" I think he takes his profession seriously and is overall a great teacher. I had lots of fun in his class and I found it challenging enough for me to use my brain and apply concepts that are taught even though AI use was allowed generously.

Ilia had a unique class structure. I've taken two classes with him and he would often times make the class read an article or discuss a current event for 20-30 minutes and spend another 5-15 minutes discussing it as a class. Taking up around 45 minutes for one current event discussion. Which only takes people 5 min to discuss.
He was okay.

Ruining first impressions for students. His marking standards and lack of teaching is reflected upon his TA's resulting in harsh marking and results. There are multiple occasions where I go to TA office hours in an attempt to dispute a grade, with no outcome because TA's lacked the directions to answer my logical questions. Delete this course.

Honestly, not a bad guy, but just very strict and for some reason hates every soul on earth. His class can be really hard to adapt to for first years, and it's a lot of self-preparing with the textbooks. Wears two watches.

Super unclear grading criteria. TA's mark almost 100% of your course work so your grade is entirely dependent on them. My TA had a clear discrepancy in grading and Ilia wouldn't listen to our complaints. would not recommend this professor.
was ok not too bad but the grading was very tough.

He seems intimidating but I promise if you go to his office hours or ask questions, he will be more than willing to help you. He understands that it is our first year (bus 201). Extremely helpful during the case competition period, and I would recommend asking if you can tips and for possible questions that can arise.

Taking this course had me wishing that on the way to lecture the bus would roll back down the mountain. His lectures are useless & attendance is mandatory; your group will be cold called. Grading, Feedback, & Final are BEYOND UNCLEAR/USELESS. AI is used for everything. DO NOT RECOMMEND RUN!
P.S. all the positive reviews about him are COMPLETE LIES!

He is a good professor, yes he has a different approach but it is enjoyable because its different. Tough grading but rewarding course in terms of knowledge. Do not procrastinate and show up to lectures and you should be fine.

This guy did not teach a single thing and his assignments are literally "how well can you use chatgpt??" The average for the final test worth 30% of our grade was a 9/20. If you like gambling, this is the perfect class for you. What grade you get is decided by him rolling a dice.

Course is self study through a textbook, Ilia barley teaches. He just yaps a lot about previous business or the news which doesn't even relate to the assignments besides one. Entire time Ilia was insanely strict and harsh. I almost lost 15% on in-class assignment when trying to submit it 15 minutes early but couldn't because canvas kept crashing.

COURSE SHOULD BE RENAMED TO CONSULTING WITH AI 201, EVERY ASSIGNMENT DOESN'T TEST YOU ON BUSINESS KNOWLEDGE BUT HOW GOOD U CAN USE CHATGPT TO MAKE REPORT IN SHORT TIMEFRAME. PICK GOOD GROUP WITH NO SLACKERS TO GET GOOD GRADES AND MAYBE PICK A RELIGION AND PRAY BEFORE EVERY WEEKLY LECTURE. GRADED HARSHLY AND YOU'LL PROBABLY GET AN AVG OF 40-60%. GL

I have never had a professor as terrible as Ilia. Every time I drive to school, I hope I crash just so I can not be in his class. His class makes me mald so hard. His criterias are impossible and encourage full use of AI in his assignments. It's basically a class of you competing with others on who can use AI the best.

This guy is one of the worst professors in SFU. His exam that is worth 30% is based on the text book that he did not teach in class. Every class he would only talk about politics. Everytime I got a graded assigment back, I wanted to jump.

GRADING FELT LIKE ROLLING DICE IN THE DARK AND I LEFT EVERY CLASS AFRAID OF FAILING. MIKE LI THE TA SHOOK ME TO MY VERY CORE BY PRODUCING HEINOUS GRADES NO CURVE COULD SAVE. LECTURES FELT LIKE PUBLIC HUMILIATION BECAUSE WHY DID ILIA PICK ON US TO SPEAK BY THREATENING OUR GRADES. I HAVE THE FURY OF A THOUSAND SUNS FROM BUS201. WORST COURSE EVER.

Lectures were basically review of chapters. Final is based on application of textbook chapters and assignment were who can produce the best AI answer. Grading was tough at start but got slightly better. Exam average was so low, so stay above curve and you'll get a decent grade. Not the worst prof ever but wouldn't say he's good.

TO ALL FIRST YEARS: BUS201 grades are luck, not knowledge. He made AI usage and even plagiarism mandatory. My TA (Mike) graded ~10% lower than other TA's. Exam avg was an F. Trust me, he was a daily stressor for me. I almost considered switching majors. Just know this/consulting isn't all of business. Btw the good ratings are written by himself

My kid attended his class and learned nothing other than to NOT think independently. Unless you are convinced that "business = use of ChatGPT", then this is not the course for you. I feel scammed for having to pay tuition for such a mandatory course that has no benefit.

honestly, Ilia is a tough marker. The class content is over complicated and he didn't really prepare us well for the final exam. He's a genuine guy tho icl, he grew on me
Nonetheless, your grade in this class will be determined on whether you have a good group or not.
 What makes a good group?: People that actually ask for feedback from the TA.

His expectations are high and he is indeed knowledgeable but the teaching quality was a mismatch. Course contains a 30% exam that was based on textbook content that he barely taught on. TAs grade extremely differently. Make sure you tackle any of the course content with hours of preparation ahead for a half-decent outcome. Avoid if possible.

You get to choose your own group during the first tutorial, so choose wisely. He also adjusts grades based on peer evaluations, so if someone doesn't pull their weight, he takes it seriously. He provides helpful, actionable feedback during office hours and genuinely supports students who put in the effort.
he didn't teach anything in class, all he did was give us an exercise with no clear instructions and told us to complete in 20 minutes

Did not learn anything in lectures, basically self study class. My TA (Mike) marked soooo hard compared to the other ones so all of our grades were always below the curve. When we tried to talk to Ilia about this he honestly did not care and told us to deal with it! Don't recommend him :)

Ilia is not a 1 star prof (still bad). putting 1 star to balance out the bots. Only reason my grade is this bad is solely because of MIKE LI my favourite ta! He marks like the curve is the max he can give. Put so much effort and time into this class, far beyond calc & others which i am getting A- and A's in. MIKE LI is a name to remember for sure.

Mostly taught how to put a prompt into chatgpt. Extremely strict with deadlines (a second late = 15% deduction). Talks about business news for most of class and will throw in business knowledge once every other lecture. Would not recommend, avoid this professor at all costs.

Take this course with any other prof. Super difficult assignment that he does not teach you how to do at ALL, this course forces you to learn everything you have to do outside the course, while he talks about his perspective on current events the whole time.

Insanely unhelpful, uses the textbooks as his way of teaching but takes up 2 hours of lectures to talk about his photography business and his love for Elon Musk. Instructions and grading unclear, his TAs are as useless as he is. He was my only option for the semester and I should've waited for the next. Good luck cuz you'll need it.

tbh after all this time idk what he is teaching everything came out from his mouth was never useful, he js yaps about nonsense, bs class. TAs all hv diff grading standard so the average is flying everywhere, RUN GUYS EVEN MY GRANDMA CAN TEACH BETTER THAN HIM.

He is a good professor. His lectures and interesting and pretty relevent to the course. However, his assignments are quite difficult and are often on the stop and timed. He has clear expectations but they are very high.

Expect 0 teaching but incredibly hard assignments, mandatory to use AI on everything (but still get a 50%), a final worth 40% that covers 13 chapters with 0% of it being taught, and finally, to learn more about business news than anything else. Please avoid this class, my grade in calculus is substantially higher than this class.

take this class if you want a crash course in how to use AI for everything (as he recommends). he could honestly be sponsored by chat gpt for the amount of mentions. your grade honestly depends on your TA. everything i've learned is about his photography business, stock updates, and tea obsession. please avoid at all costs ! like seriously RUN

Ilia's class is hard because the curve is very tight. The outline is a bit weird but you can see Ilia is a smart prof and is knowledgeable about the economy. He should teach economics, it would suit his character better. Goodluck on your second try. lmfao

I hope Ilia reads this and changes his "teaching", because for a business professor, what kind of value does he add to my educational experience? The exam is all self-taught and he doesn't have an accommodating personality as a first year lecturer.
Would never recommend this professor

Worst prof in beedie so far.

- All he does is talk about global news rather than teaching how to write better.
- Unnecessary thinking fast presentations
- Reviewed the group assignment with the TA, she said it was good and when she marks it, gives my team a bad mark
- Never take it with him
does not lecture, you need to learn all content on your one, lack of empathy, he is not human

Took 360 with him in the summer. Not sure why all the first years are complaining. Fifteen chapters is normal for a university course. The prof goes over the main ideas and you read the rest yourself, which is how university works. Class averages are low, but the curve will save you. If you go to him for feedback, he actually gives helpful advice.

Lectures are useless cuz he just talks about business news or does in class assignments. A big portion of the class is group work with your tutorial group so find good people. TAs mark very differently so if you're unlucky and get a TA that marks super hard good luck. You have to read a textbook of 15 chapters but never goes over it in class.
He's based. Honestly think the 1 star reviews are from really soft people lol

I have never thought the new freshman beedie course could be hard like this; this instructor is so bad and not even teaching anything, just caring about his useless news. 50% average could imagine what his class looks like. Avoid Avoid Avoid!

He never teach you anything about the textbook but the test requires u to know everything from the text book. It's way too much for a first year uni student, also really hard time doing in class assignments, running out of time. Class average 50, TA mark really strict. Good luck with reading the whole textbook with 15 chapters, and presentations.
bad prof. don't take. avoid avoid avoid. he should've stuck to photography.

run while u still can. doesn't teach at all. i don't understand why we have to learn business news in class????? all self taught. says he doesn't have enough time to cover content BUT HE LITERALLY WOULD IF HE JUST TAUGHT INSTEAD OF MAKING US DO STUPID 45 MIN PREP PRESENTATIONS AND SHARING NEWS.

He is not clear with what he wants and your grade will reflect that. I asked him what format he prefers for an in-class group assaignment you have to do in 50 minutes and he said "its not what I prefer, its what the human brain prefers". All he talks about in lectures are things that wont even be applied later on in the course.

This is a self taught course. The lectures do not help whatsoever. If anyone is reading this, this course probably uses the textbook "Business Essentials Canadian Edition", Find the most up to date one and read it. Also, if you haven't used AI before, start practicing. The assignments gives you very little time.

Try to get another prof. Good luck.

Knowledgeable prof but u self-teach urself, get ready to read 15 textbook chapters + know most of concepts.

Split between individual assignments // group projects with exam worth 30%, choose a good group. 

Graded way too strictly (pissed me off more than calc I) get ready to receive 40-60s, but course is curved (70+ is A).

good luck 08's not fun

studying for the exam worth 30% of our grade rn and i actually might jump. he taught 0 material so you need to study entirely on your own. could take this whole class without him even existing since you do everything yourself and he makes his tas mark it all for him. run while you still can. i'm serious.
Horrible teacher and a tough grader. Would highly recommend to avoid this teacher at all costs.

Very fun and inspiring course! Illia clearly explains how to prepare for assignments and offers great tips. The workload is average, and lectures deepen understanding of pre-readings. Assignments are graded fairly with helpful feedback. Overall, Illia's class was an amazing first university experience that fueled my passion for business.

He assigns at least 2 chapters to read every week. In lecture/ assignments, he will be discussing business cases with the concept in textbook that he doesn't teach and expect us to know. He let us use AI only because he gives very little time in class/ tut that no one can finish the assignment without the help of AI.

Had him for BUS 201, would not recommend. If any other prof available immediately take them over Ilia. 30% final exam on 15 chapters at the end of October. 15% from group paper with only 40 minutes to complete. Allowed to use AI for most things, but won't help you achieve a good grade.

This professor is quite strict at marking despite his unclear rubrics in his classes. He makes you read the textbook and study at home (which is fine), however, he does not mention them on lectures at all and is completely useless because he doesn't assess your learning with quizzes. He TALKS ABOUT NEWS AND NEVER TEACHES HOW TO EVEN ANALYZE THEM
Dedicated and enthusiastic, but unclear and inconsistent; slow feedback and slide-heavy lectures. More structure and hands-on work would make a big difference.
his assignments are genuinely just a test on who can use AI better. didn't learn a thing. waste of money
do not take this course with this proof, he has useless lectures, tutorials are all writing assignments and there is no time to prepare for them ahead of time. super hard marking.
He's a vibes professor if you want structure on assignment he's not the guy.

Great class. I would take it again. He was amazing. He made the class a lot better than most professors. His WDC assignments are a really great exercise. Don't be shy to ask for help, as he gives very concise feedback.

He doesn't actually teach the material,
 and the class is extremely stressful. 
We were only given about 40 minutes to prepare presentations during class. He was unprofessional and biased toward Russian students, making the class environment very unpleasant.

LOVED ilia for 217. took him in the summer, and he was amazing. There are a couple of group assignments, so be sure to pick a good group. Feedback and instructions weren't really clear on the WDC, but overall he's a really nice and understanding prof if you get to know him :)

Do not take the class with him. Has a bunch of assignments that are like pop quizzes you get no context ahead of time and you go to tutorial and just start writing. The other sections of this class have an advantage because it's taught by another teacher. They get their assignment info a week ahead so they have time to prepare. Please beware!!

Absolutely amazing professor! The class was moderately difficult, but it rewards the time and effort you put in. Ilia's engaging and supportive teaching style makes the material clear and helps students succeed. Truly one of the best professors at SFU Beedie!

Ilia is a very nice prof! His lectures can be quite dry, but they give you time to work on group projects or prepare for WDC assignments that are taken in tutorials. You have less than 50 mins to complete them, and the feedback given was not helpful. You can use chat in this course, but Ilia states that to get an A or B, you need to go beyond chat.

Notoriously difficult class, somehow made worse by Ilia. The only content to learn is in the survival guide. Lectures are just discussions and opinions; I learned nothing. I had challenges with group members, Ilia did not help, and my grade suffered.
Political discussions surrounding Biden, Trump, and Elon were part of every class. Terrible.
I don't know how he could still teach in the university, he should go to teach "Early Education"! Oh sorry I forgot, little kids don't like "In the News!"
I have ever seen such a bad instructor! Always to talk useless things (even not know how to do well in this course, read slides and in the news, in the news....
Ended with 74% but got downward curved to a C because of the rank based system.

This was a very annoying class in general. I did well on the individual assignments but my group dragged my mark through the mud. He never taught anything about writing in class and then expected us to do things like write 2 whole pages cited with 3 different sources in less than 50 mins. Also the TA docked points for things we were never taught.

Ilia is a chill prof and he is very approachable. bus360 was so much easier than I expected. Lots of submissions, but it's manageable (217 and 303 were way worse). Try to find a good team, and you'll be fine. TA's expectations are high, so critical thinking and attention to detail are important. I would definitely take this class with Ilia again.

Save urself the stress & take this class w/ a different prof. I don't think I've genuinely learned anything in this class…all we've talked about is “business news” (ramble abt Elon). The criteria for each assignment is very vague. The readings are meant for the assignments but they usually end up being useless or the prof doesn't even go over them.

He doesn't care or delve into the outlines for any assignments so you are unsure about what you need to even do to get a good grade. He spends a lot of time talking about random stuff that is unrelated to the course. There is a written exam that is graded pretty harshly to watch for as well.

The prof never actually talked about the course material in class or taught anything. He kept on talking about world news and asked us to discuss about it. He's not a tough prof tho. I went through the textbook for the exams and they weren't that difficult.
Business news discussions are useful
Not recommended overall; avoid choosing another instructor for this course. Waste of money; pay more efforts and get such a low grade :(

This is the type of class you take for the credit and not because you plan to learn anything. You will not learn anything, honestly your grade is almost entirely reliant on how good the group members you are placed with are. The only time the prof talks is when he speaks about current news stories. 

If this class is not for credit don't take lol

Honestly, he was great. He wants you to give the taste of the real world and how BUS360 will be. All the people giving him a 1, look at their grades, putting all the blame on the TAs and the prof. I'd 100% take him again for BUS217w. He's quite knowledgeable and if you think he writes his own reviews, well all the best lol! Really get a grip guys

If you love Elon Musk, you will have lots to talk about with Ilia. Overall didn't feel like I learnt anything from the instructor, classes were an *open discussion* most of the time. (He loves to talk about himself) TAs are on another level, and grading is extremely tough and punitive, for even some things outside of your control : (

Ilia's class was overall good. The assignments were interesting as you got to apply the material to realistic examples to build your understanding. The coursework is graded hard, but it's consistent. The biggest downside is being stuck with a poor group, where the assignments are worth 50%. I think the group work should be worth less.
Take it with any other prof.

Ilia talks a lot about random things during lectures. BUS 217W in itself is a very weird course; it doesn't really teach you anything. Most classes are spent listening to pointless current event lectures as the try-hard students yap with Ilia for 2 hours. Projects are timed/speed-based with TAs docking marks for every small error.

Ilia really is a good prof, he is heavy on class activities but also provides valuable insights for your work. The key is to use the feedback and use it in your work. He's happy to answer all the questions, but it's not a regular class, it's heavily based on your actually learning rather than the tests, which is I suppose gonna help irl?!

Trust me when I say it, he was the best prof I've had here in SFU, really wants you to succeed and gives amazing feedback The class is designed in a way that you have to learn the course concepts and use them in real life situations or do the implementations. It's all about how good you are with active learning. It's not him writing this lol.

If you LOVE tesla and elon musk take this class! If you have any other interests in life and love happiness, your well being, and hope - DO NOT! This was the worst class I have ever taken in my university experience, possibly even my entire education career path. Please take 217 with anyone else! Susan Christie Bell is a saint in comparison!!!!!!!!
He is awesome, but the course is not an easy way to get a higher mark! Just choose this section without any doubts!
TA's of the class are such hard markers, you will do so much work just to get a bad work. bro showed up 10 mins late once and did not say nothing. Talks for most of the class. just dont take it w him

A lot of people like him BUT he does not go over anything to do with the course, assignment expectations or teach anything in class really. It's mostly just a yap session for him about current events. He only gives brief guidelines AFTER the assignments on how to improve. Idk what I learned...just to type fast.

A very stringent marker whose grading criteria is nothing short of obscure. Exam isnt from anything of the classes but rather strictly from the textbook-which we never go through. A lot of papers with a 2000+ word count.

Do not take this professor. He will ruin the way you approach school. The strenuous assignments will destroy you. He forces you to read textbook content that he doesn't talk about in lectures. He also makes no effort in providing constructive feedback catered to your learning as he is lazy. Only take this course if you want to lower your GPA.

He provides such vague guidelines in the rubric and then explains what he wanted after handing back the marked assignments. Lectures are extremely boring with no proper format to teach anything before he sends you off to hastily put together a presentation with your group that prepares you for nothing.

If you enjoy keeping up to date with economics or politics, this is the class for you. Professor Bykov allows an environment for open dialogue on a broad range of subjects, which is complemented nicely by his knowledge on a vast array of topics. Despite the lower grade, this has been my favourite class (and professor) in university thus far.

Ilia's class is so much fun as long as you follow up and do the work, I can certainly say it was one of my favourite classes. I was so happy each day to wake up and go to his class. He created a respectful and calm environment for everyone. He inspired me a lot in both life and educational aspect. Definitely never seen a professor as caring as him!
I love this instructor, nice!

Ilia's course has quickly become one of my all-time favorites! The lectures are filled with practical and useful content. I loved the In The News segment at the beginning of each class. The assignments are manageable if you read the material beforehand. The grading system is fair, the feedback provided is constructive

Ilia is the best professor ever! His teaching style promotes interesting academic advancement, and by blending business theory with current events, he brings the subject matter to life, making every lesson a unique and practical learning experience. Ilia also provides useful feedback, pointing out areas where a student could make improvements.
Amazing prof. With an engaging teaching style and deep industry knowledge, they create an enriching learning environment that makes complex business concepts accessible and enjoyable.

Prof is good the way he teaches but tbh he will never teach anything in class which he will ask in exams, so start reading yourself and from the textbooks. He also is a tough grader and might give more marks to female students. Participation is a must and his classes are enjoyable. Only Downside: Tough Grading!!!
Not recommended overall; teach nothing in the class but test the textbook in the midterm.
If you don't teach anything in class, the students don't know where the points are deducted. He's not fit to teach. Choose another professor rather than him....
Do not take this course with him!!!!!!!! He is so terrible. He doesn't teach anything in class and he is very intimidating.
